---
title: "VII — Complete systems"
description: "System studies that integrate models, evidence, trade-offs, and handover."
page-role: part
part-id: systems
---

The system studies reverse the usual chapter perspective. Instead of isolating
one concept, they begin with a need and ask which representations, interfaces,
budgets, risks, and evidence must agree for the whole system to be credible.

## Part question

What changes when analog, digital, embedded, power, communication, mechanical,
human, and lifecycle decisions must succeed together?

## Entry knowledge

These are synthesis chapters. Each has explicit prerequisites in the chapter
registry, and each expects the record-keeping practices in [T03](../appendices/t03-reproducible-engineering.qmd).
A reader may enter one study after its prerequisites rather than waiting to
finish every domain chapter.

## Learning arc

| Chapters | Development |
|---|---|
| X01 | integrate precision analog, conversion, calibration, and uncertainty |
| X02 | balance sensing, logging, power, storage, enclosure, and recovery |
| X03 | connect programmable hardware, communication, threat modeling, and secure recovery |
| X04 | adapt a maintainable system to field context, infrastructure, users, and service constraints |
| X05 | qualify, defend, hand over, monitor, and improve a complete system |

## Exit capability

You should be able to maintain a system-level argument from need to evidence,
allocate requirements across interfaces, identify a cross-domain failure,
resolve conflicting trade-offs transparently, and prepare a handover that lets
another competent team reproduce and improve the result.

The studies align with projects M3–M8 in the
[practice and project spine](../roadmap.qmd#practice-and-project-spine). The
project artifact is the evidence; the study explains how to reason about it and
bound the resulting claim.
